newsSince: Two new June items. Positive: on June 2 Infineon launched system-level AI data-center power solutions (18kW/30kW PSU designs) and joined NVIDIA's MGX 800VDC ecosystem, reiterating €1.5B FY26 / €2.5B FY27 AI data-center revenue targets ahead of the July 2 Dresden Module 4 opening. Negative: China's Supreme People's Court upheld a sales injunction barring Infineon's GaN products in China (Innoscience patents; ~RMB10M damages), which Infineon calls 'very limited' as only a small slice of its GaN portfolio is affected.

sources.label: China Supreme Court GaN injunction (Tom's Hardware)

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha-vs-passives lens: the June 2 AI-PSU launches + NVIDIA MGX 800VDC win reinforce the power-delivery thesis (€1.5B FY26 / €2.5B FY27 AI target), modestly offset by the China GaN ban (company: 'very limited'). Net mildly positive but ~110% YTD caps residual alpha. Hold 20, +1.
verdicts.bull: Direct AI-rack power-delivery winner; NVIDIA 800VDC ecosystem; €2.5B FY27 AI target; Dresden M4 ramps July.
verdicts.neutral: AI-power catalysts real but partly offset by China GaN ban; large-cap so residual alpha is thin.
verdicts.bear: GaN China injunction signals IP/competitive risk; +110% YTD leaves little un-priced alpha for a passives-beating list.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI data-center power ramp (NVIDIA MGX, Dresden M4) hitting €2.5B FY27
verdicts.primaryRisk: Auto/industrial cycle weakness; GaN IP losses widening beyond China

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent AI-node lens: new AI data-center PSU designs and the NVIDIA MGX 800VDC membership strengthen the power-conversion bottleneck thesis; GaN China ban is a contained offset. Too large/known for max early alpha, so +1 only. Hold rank 64.
verdicts.bull: Power-conversion content per AI rack rising; concrete 18-30kW PSU reference designs; €1.5B FY26 AI revenue.
verdicts.neutral: Genuine node exposure but mega-cap and well-covered; GaN setback trims the wide-bandgap optionality.
verdicts.bear: Too large/known for latent-alpha purity; GaN China exclusion dents a 58%-growth product line.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: 800VDC/AI rack power ramp; Dresden Module 4 silicon-MOSFET ramp
verdicts.primaryRisk: China GaN exclusion plus auto/industrial cyclicality

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node bottleneck lens: analog/power/RF tier is less direct than HBM/packaging/test, but June's AI-PSU launches + NVIDIA 800VDC win sharpen the AI-rack power claim. GaN China ban is a modest offset. Net +1; not enough to outrank more direct bottleneck names. Hold 49.
verdicts.bull: Credible AI-rack power content with named NVIDIA design wins; €2.5B FY27 AI target.
verdicts.neutral: Power is adjacent to, not the core of, the HBM/packaging compute bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: Less direct than memory/packaging names; GaN China ban and auto cycle weigh.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI data-center power-delivery design wins ramping to volume

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO/optical-interconnect lens: Infineon is a power-density beneficiary, not an optical-interconnect pure play. June's news is all power/PSU and GaN-IP, none of which touches co-packaged optics. No change to the CPO thesis. Hold rank 69.
verdicts.bull: Higher rack power density indirectly raises the value of efficient power delivery alongside optical links.
verdicts.neutral: Off-axis for a CPO lens; power content is real but not the optical bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: Not an optical-interconnect play; GaN China ban adds noise without CPO relevance.
verdicts.primaryRisk: No CPO exposure; auto/industrial cycle and GaN IP risk

verdicts.rankRationale: Under-owned-bottleneck master list (board-level power/passives): June AI-PSU launches + NVIDIA MGX win validate the AI-server PDN thesis; GaN China ban is a contained offset (company: 'very limited'). Mildly positive but +110% YTD makes it less hidden. Hold rank 76, +1.
verdicts.bull: AI server PDN/power-semi content scaling with 800VDC racks; concrete NVIDIA ecosystem role; €2.5B FY27 AI target.
verdicts.neutral: Bottleneck exposure intact but increasingly recognized; GaN setback offsets some upside.
verdicts.bear: Less-hidden mega-cap after big re-rating; GaN China exclusion; auto/industrial pricing risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI server power-delivery ramp (Dresden M4, NVIDIA 800VDC)
verdicts.primaryRisk: GaN IP losses and auto/industrial cycle pressuring power-semi execution
